summary: cursed-modules exfiltrates installer secrets and ships C2 relay at install time
details: package.json declares preinstall/install/postinstall all running `node install.js`, causing the malicious code path to fire automatically on `npm install`. install.js reads candidate flag files (/flag, /root/flag, /proc/self/environ), executes `cat /flag*`, and POSTs results with the hostname to local relay ports. recon.js harvests the full process environment, the process and network state, `~/.npmrc` and `/root/.npmrc`, verdaccio htpasswd/config files, and home/app/root directory listings, then PUTs the bundle as JSON to a hardcoded external endpoint at http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-654321 with a curl fallback. rce.sh implements a tasked command-and-control relay: it PUTs shell commands through http://154.57.164.70:32447/api/modules/ECT-654321 and reads base64-encoded command output from /api/modules/ECT-987654, forming a remote command channel that shares module IDs with install.js. publish-and-arm.sh ships automation that runs `npm publish --access public` and registers a manifest tagging the package for dependency-confusion / verdaccio-proxy abuse against the same attacker endpoint. The package combines installer credential theft, host data exfiltration to attacker infrastructure, a remote command backdoor, and dependency-confusion publishing tooling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
